medications.drug_name: hydrocortisone 1% cream (mild topical corticosteroid) to surrounding eczematous skin
medications.indication_rationale: Reduces the surrounding itch/eczematous reaction and discomfort; apply to the red flat skin around the bumps.
medications.dose_route_frequency: apply twice daily to surrounding red skin (as per source note)

medications.drug_name: zinc oxide barrier cream + simple analgesia (paracetamol) as needed
medications.indication_rationale: Barrier reduces friction/pain on walking; analgesia for discomfort.

medications.drug_name: systemic antibiotics for the BOTE reaction
medications.indication_rationale: BOTE is an immune (not bacterial) reaction — antibiotics are unnecessary unless genuine secondary infection develops.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Keep the child's nails short and hands washed, don't squeeze/pick the bumps, and use loose clothing/barrier cream to reduce friction.

patient_education_points.point: These are molluscum — harmless viral bumps. The sudden redness (the 'BOTE' sign) actually means your child's immune system has found the virus and is clearing it — it's a good sign, not a spreading infection.
We only treat the itch and friction — a mild steroid cream to the red skin around the bumps, a barrier cream, and pain relief if needed. Don't squeeze the bumps.
If the redness becomes hard and hot and spreads down the leg, a big painful pocket of pus forms, or your child develops a fever — seek medical care.

follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Spreading hot/hard redness, a pus pocket, or fever — seek urgent/emergency care.

must_include_items: Recognise inflamed molluscum (BOTE sign) rather than cellulitis and AVOID unnecessary systemic antibiotics
Screen for true secondary bacterial infection
Reassure parents; treat itch (mild topical steroid) and friction (barrier), short nails/hygiene, no squeezing
acceptable_alternatives: Simple emollient instead of/with barrier cream
errors_of_commission: Prescribing systemic antibiotics for the BOTE reaction
Sending a benign BOTE to ED
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires recognising BOTE (no antibiotics), screening for true infection, reassurance/itch-friction management, and a bacterial-infection safety-net.
